records.ety: [OF. testifier, L. testificari; testis a witness + -ficare (in comp.) to make. See -fy, and cf. Attest, Contest, Detest, Protest, Testament.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Testified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Testifying (?).]

defs: To make a solemn declaration, verbal or written, to establish some fact; to give testimony for the purpose of communicating to others a knowledge of something not known to them.
quotes: Jesus . . . needed not that any should testify of man, for he knew what was in man.

defs: To make a solemn declaration under oath or affirmation, for the purpose of establishing, or making proof of, some fact to a court; to give testimony in a cause depending before a tribunal.
quotes: One witness shall not testify against any person to cause him to die.

defs: To declare a charge; to protest; to give information; to bear witness; -- with against.
quotes: O Israel, . . . I will testify against thee.
I testified against them in the day wherein they sold victuals.

defs: To bear witness to; to support the truth of by testimony; to affirm or declare solemnly.
quotes: We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.

defs: To affirm or declare under oath or affirmation before a tribunal, in order to prove some fact.
In a testy manner; fretfully; peevishly; with petulance.
